exercises.description: Lie on a flat bench. Grip the bar slightly wider than shoulder-width. Unrack the bar and lower it to your mid-chest. Press the bar back up until your arms are extended. Squeeze your chest at the top.
exercises.common_errors: 1. Bouncing the bar off your chest 2. Flaring elbows too wide 3. Arching your back excessively

exercises.description: Lie on a flat bench holding dumbbells above your chest. Lower the dumbbells to the sides of your chest until your elbows are at 90 degrees. Press the dumbbells back up to the starting position. Squeeze your chest at the top.
exercises.common_errors: 1. Going too heavy and losing form 2. Not lowering enough 3. Dumbbells colliding at the top

exercises.description: Set an adjustable bench to a 30-45 degree incline. Lie back and grip the bar slightly wider than shoulder-width. Lower the bar to your upper chest. Press the bar up and back until arms are extended.
exercises.common_errors: 1. Using too much incline (over 45 deg) 2. Not keeping shoulders packed 3. Pressing straight up instead of up and back

exercises.description: Set the bench to a 15-30 degree decline. Lie back and secure your feet. Grip the bar slightly wider than shoulder-width. Lower the bar to your lower chest. Press up to full arm extension.
exercises.common_errors: 1. Using too much decline 2. Letting the bar drift too low 3. Overarching the neck

exercises.description: Lie on a flat bench holding dumbbells above your chest with a slight bend in your elbows. Lower your arms out to the sides in a wide arc until you feel a stretch in your chest. Bring the dumbbells back together using your chest muscles. Squeeze at the top.
exercises.common_errors: 1. Bending your elbows too much (becoming a press) 2. Using too much weight 3. Lowering too far and injuring the shoulder

exercises.description: Set both pulleys to chest height. Stand in the middle and grab each handle. Step forward slightly and lean forward. With a slight bend in your elbows, bring your hands together in front of your chest. Squeeze your chest for a moment before slowly returning to the start.
exercises.common_errors: 1. Using too much weight 2. Not keeping your shoulders back 3. Bending and extending your elbows through the movement

exercises.description: Start in a high plank position with your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width. Lower your body until your chest nearly touches the floor. Keep your body in a straight line from head to heels. Push back up to the starting position. Squeeze your chest at the top.
exercises.common_errors: 1. Sagging your hips 2. Flaring your elbows too wide 3. Not going through full range of motion

exercises.description: Grip the parallel bars and lift yourself up. Lean your torso forward slightly to target your chest more. Lower your body by bending your elbows until your upper arms are parallel to the floor. Push yourself back up to the starting position. Control the movement throughout.
exercises.common_errors: 1. Staying too upright (becomes triceps exercise) 2. Going too low and injuring the shoulder 3. Swinging or kipping

exercises.description: Sit in the machine and adjust the seat so the handles are at chest height. Press the handles forward until your arms are fully extended. Keep your back against the pad and your chest up. Slowly return to the starting position under control.
exercises.common_errors: 1. Setting the seat too high or low 2. Shrugging your shoulders 3. Returning the weight too fast

exercises.description: Sit in the pec deck machine with your forearms against the pads and your elbows at shoulder height. Bring your hands together in front of your chest by squeezing your chest muscles. Hold the squeeze for a moment. Slowly return to the starting position.
exercises.common_errors: 1. Using your triceps to push 2. Using too much weight 3. Lifting your shoulders off the pad

exercises.description: Grip the bar with palms facing away, slightly wider than shoulder-width. Hang with your arms fully extended. Pull yourself up until your chin clears the bar. Squeeze your lats at the top. Lower yourself under control back to the starting position.
exercises.common_errors: 1. Kipping or swinging 2. Not going to full dead hang 3. Shrugging your shoulders at the top

exercises.description: Grip the bar with palms facing you, shoulder-width apart. Hang with your arms extended. Pull yourself up until your chin is over the bar. Squeeze your back at the top. Lower yourself under control back to the start.
exercises.common_errors: 1. Swinging your body 2. Using too much bicep 3. Not lowering fully

exercises.description: Sit at the lat pulldown machine and secure your thighs. Grip the bar with palms facing away, wider than shoulder-width. Pull the bar down to your upper chest while squeezing your shoulder blades together. Slowly return the bar to the starting position.
exercises.common_errors: 1. Leaning too far back 2. Pulling the bar behind your neck 3. Returning too fast

exercises.description: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart holding a barbell with an overhand grip. Hinge at your hips until your torso is at about a 45-degree angle to the floor. Pull the bar to your lower ribcage, squeezing your shoulder blades together. Lower the bar under control.
exercises.common_errors: 1. Rounding your lower back 2. Using your arms to pull instead of your back 3. Jerking the weight up

exercises.description: Place one knee and hand on a bench. Hold a dumbbell in your other hand with your arm extended. Pull the dumbbell toward your hip, squeezing your back muscles at the top. Lower the dumbbell under control. Keep your back flat throughout.
exercises.common_errors: 1. Rotating your torso 2. Shrugging at the top 3. Using momentum

exercises.description: Sit at the cable row station and place your feet on the platform. Grab the V-handle and sit up tall with your chest out. Pull the handle to your stomach, squeezing your shoulder blades together. Slowly return to the starting position. Keep your back straight.
exercises.common_errors: 1. Leaning too far forward and back 2. Rounding your shoulders 3. Using too much arm strength

exercises.description: Set a cable pulley to upper-chest height. Grab the rope attachment with both hands. Step back and hold the rope in front of your face with arms extended. Pull the rope toward your face, separating the ends. Squeeze your rear delts at the peak. Slowly return.
exercises.common_errors: 1. Using too much weight 2. Not pulling to the face level 3. Leaning back

exercises.description: Stand with your mid-foot under the barbell. Bend at your hips and knees to grip the bar. Keep your back straight and chest up. Drive through your heels to stand up with the bar. Lower the bar by pushing your hips back first.
exercises.common_errors: 1. Rounding your lower back 2. Bar drifting away from your body 3. Jerking the bar off the floor

exercises.description: Hold a barbell at hip height with an overhand grip. With a slight bend in your knees, push your hips back and lower the bar along your legs. Lower until you feel a stretch in your hamstrings. Drive your hips forward to return to the starting position.
exercises.common_errors: 1. Bending your knees too much (becomes squat) 2. Rounding your back 3. Letting the bar drift away from your legs

exercises.description: Position yourself face down on a hyperextension bench with your hips on the pad. Cross your arms over your chest. Lower your upper body toward the floor while keeping your back straight. Raise your torso back up until your body forms a straight line. Squeeze your glutes at the top.
exercises.common_errors: 1. Over-extending at the top 2. Going too fast 3. Hyperextending your neck

exercises.description: Position the bar on your upper back (not your neck). Stand with feet shoulder-width apart. Brace your core and bend your knees and hips to lower into a squat. Descend until your thighs are at least parallel to the floor. Drive through your heels to stand back up.
exercises.common_errors: 1. Letting your knees cave in 2. Lifting your heels 3. Rounding your lower back

exercises.description: Position the bar across the front of your shoulders with your elbows high. Keep your torso upright as you squat down. Descend until your thighs are parallel to the floor. Drive up through your heels to stand. Keep your elbows up throughout to prevent the bar from rolling forward.
exercises.common_errors: 1. Letting your elbows drop 2. Falling forward 3. Not going deep enough

exercises.description: Hold one end of a dumbbell with both hands against your chest. Stand with your feet slightly wider than shoulder-width. Squat down, keeping your torso upright and your elbows between your knees at the bottom. Drive up through your heels to stand.
exercises.common_errors: 1. Letting your knees cave in 2. Rising onto your toes 3. Falling forward

exercises.description: Sit in the leg press machine and place your feet on the platform shoulder-width apart. Lower the platform by bending your knees to 90 degrees. Press through your entire foot to extend your legs. Do not lock your knees at the top.
exercises.common_errors: 1. Locking your knees at the top 2. Lowering too far 3. Taking your feet too high or low

exercises.description: Sit in the leg extension machine with your ankles behind the pads. Extend your legs until they are straight. Squeeze your quadriceps at the top. Lower the weight under control. Do not let the weight slam down.
exercises.common_errors: 1. Using momentum 2. Extending too fast 3. Not going through full range

exercises.description: Lie face down on the hamstring curl machine with the pad behind your ankles. Curl your legs toward your glutes by bending your knees. Squeeze your hamstrings at the top. Lower the weight under control. Keep your hips pressed into the pad.
exercises.common_errors: 1. Using momentum 2. Lifting your hips off the pad 3. Not curling fully

exercises.description: Stand with your feet hip-width apart holding dumbbells at your sides. Step forward with one leg and lower your hips until both knees are at about 90 degrees. Your front knee should be above your ankle. Push through your front heel to return to standing. Repeat on the other leg.
exercises.common_errors: 1. Front knee traveling past your toes 2. Leaning forward 3. Taking too short a step

exercises.description: Stand about two feet in front of a bench. Place the top of one foot on the bench behind you. Hold a dumbbell in each hand. Lower your back knee toward the floor until your front thigh is parallel to the floor. Drive through your front heel to stand.
exercises.common_errors: 1. Front knee traveling past your toes too far 2. Leaning forward 3. Losing balance

exercises.description: Lie on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the floor. Press through your heels to lift your hips toward the ceiling. Squeeze your glutes at the top. Hold for a moment before lowering your hips almost to the floor.
exercises.common_errors: 1. Lifting too high and arching your back 2. Pushing through your toes 3. Going too fast

exercises.description: Stand on a calf raise machine with your shoulders under the pads. Place the balls of your feet on the step. Lower your heels down until you feel a stretch in your calves. Push through the balls of your feet to raise your heels as high as possible. Squeeze at the top.
exercises.common_errors: 1. Going too fast 2. Not getting a full stretch 3. Bending your knees during the movement

exercises.description: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart holding a barbell at shoulder level. Press the bar overhead until your arms are fully extended. Keep your core tight and avoid arching your back. Lower the bar back to shoulder level under control.
exercises.common_errors: 1. Arching your back excessively 2. Pressing the bar in front of your face 3. Not locking out at the top

exercises.description: Sit on a bench with back support holding dumbbells at shoulder height. Press the dumbbells overhead until your arms are fully extended. Keep your core tight. Lower the dumbbells back to shoulder height. Keep the movement controlled.
exercises.common_errors: 1. Arching your back 2. Pressing asymmetrically 3. Flaring your elbows too wide

exercises.description: Sit on a bench holding dumbbells in front of your shoulders with palms facing you. Press the dumbbells overhead while rotating your palms to face forward. At the top, your palms should face forward. Reverse the motion to lower back to the start.
exercises.common_errors: 1. Rotating too fast 2. Using too much weight 3. Arching your lower back

exercises.description: Stand holding a light dumbbell in each hand at your sides. With a slight bend in your elbows, raise the dumbbells out to your sides until they reach shoulder height. Keep your elbows slightly higher than your wrists. Lower back down under control.
exercises.common_errors: 1. Shrugging your traps 2. Using momentum 3. Raising too high

exercises.description: Stand holding dumbbells in front of your thighs. With a slight bend in your elbows, raise the dumbbells forward and up until they reach shoulder height. Lower them back down under control. Keep your body still throughout.
exercises.common_errors: 1. Swinging your body 2. Raising too high 3. Shrugging your shoulders

exercises.description: Hinge at your hips until your torso is almost parallel to the floor. Hold a light dumbbell in each hand hanging below your chest. With a slight bend in your elbows, raise your arms out to the sides until they are in line with your body. Squeeze your rear delts. Lower under control.
exercises.common_errors: 1. Rounding your back 2. Using too much weight 3. Bending your elbows too much

exercises.description: Sit facing the pec deck machine with your chest against the pad. Grab the handles with your arms extended in front of you. Pull your arms back and out to the sides, squeezing your rear delts. Slowly return to the start.
exercises.common_errors: 1. Using too much weight 2. Bending your elbows excessively 3. Shrugging your shoulders

exercises.description: Stand holding a barbell with an overhand grip at arm's length in front of your thighs. Pull the bar up along your body toward your chin, leading with your elbows. Keep the bar close to your body. Lower the bar back to the starting position.
exercises.common_errors: 1. Grip too narrow, causing wrist strain 2. Pulling too high 3. Swinging the weight up

exercises.description: Stand holding a barbell in front of your thighs with an overhand grip. Shrug your shoulders straight up toward your ears as high as possible. Hold the peak contraction for a moment. Lower your shoulders back down under control.
exercises.common_errors: 1. Rolling your shoulders instead of shrugging straight up 2. Using your arms to help 3. Not getting full range of motion

exercises.description: Stand holding a dumbbell in each hand at your sides. Shrug your shoulders straight up toward your ears. Squeeze at the top. Lower your shoulders back down under control. Do not roll your shoulders.
exercises.common_errors: 1. Rolling your shoulders 2. Bending your elbows 3. Going too fast

exercises.description: Stand holding a barbell with an underhand grip at shoulder width. Keeping your elbows pinned to your sides, curl the bar up toward your shoulders. Squeeze your biceps at the top. Lower the bar under control back to the starting position.
exercises.common_errors: 1. Swinging your body 2. Moving your elbows forward 3. Lowering too fast

exercises.description: Stand holding dumbbells at your sides with palms facing forward. Keeping your elbows pinned to your sides, curl the dumbbells up toward your shoulders. Squeeze your biceps at the top. Lower the dumbbells under control.
exercises.common_errors: 1. Swinging your body 2. Moving your elbows forward 3. Not curling fully

exercises.description: Stand holding dumbbells at your sides with palms facing each other. Keeping your elbows pinned to your sides, curl the dumbbells up toward your shoulders. Keep your palms facing each other throughout. Squeeze at the top. Lower under control.
exercises.common_errors: 1. Swinging your body 2. Rotating your wrists 3. Going too fast

exercises.description: Sit on a bench with your legs spread. Hold a dumbbell in one hand and rest the back of that arm against your inner thigh. Curl the dumbbell up toward your shoulder. Squeeze your biceps at the top. Lower under control.
exercises.common_errors: 1. Leaning back 2. Letting your elbow come off your thigh 3. Using momentum

exercises.description: Stand at a cable machine with a straight bar attached to the high pulley. Grip the bar with palms facing down, elbows at your sides. Push the bar down until your arms are fully extended. Squeeze your triceps at the bottom. Return under control.
exercises.common_errors: 1. Leaning forward 2. Letting your elbows move away from your body 3. Returning the weight too fast

exercises.description: Lie on a flat bench holding a barbell or EZ bar above your chest with arms extended. Keeping your upper arms still, bend your elbows to lower the bar toward your forehead. Extend your arms to return to the starting position.
exercises.common_errors: 1. Flaring your elbows 2. Moving your upper arms 3. Using too much weight

exercises.description: Lie on a flat bench and grip the barbell with hands shoulder-width apart or closer. Lower the bar to your lower chest. Keep your elbows close to your body throughout. Press the bar back up to full extension.
exercises.common_errors: 1. Grip too narrow causing wrist pain 2. Flaring your elbows 3. Shrugging at the top

exercises.description: Place your hands on the edge of a bench, fingers facing forward. Walk your feet out and lower your body by bending your elbows to about 90 degrees. Push yourself back up to the starting position. Keep your back close to the bench.
exercises.common_errors: 1. Lowering too far and straining the shoulder 2. Moving too far from the bench 3. Going too fast

exercises.description: Sit on a bench holding one dumbbell with both hands over your head. Keeping your upper arms close to your ears, lower the dumbbell behind your head. Extend your arms to return the weight overhead. Squeeze your triceps at the top.
exercises.common_errors: 1. Flaring your elbows 2. Hitting your head with the weight 3. Arching your lower back

exercises.description: Stand holding a barbell with an overhand (palms down) grip at shoulder width. Keeping your elbows at your sides, curl the bar up toward your shoulders. Squeeze at the top. Lower under control.
exercises.common_errors: 1. Using your body to swing the weight 2. Moving your elbows 3. Bending your wrists

exercises.description: Start on your hands and knees. Lower onto your forearms with elbows directly under your shoulders. Extend your legs back, balancing on your toes. Keep your body in a straight line from head to heels. Hold the position, keeping your core tight.
exercises.common_errors: 1. Sagging your hips 2. Lifting your hips too high 3. Holding your breath

exercises.description: Lie on your back with knees bent and feet flat on the floor. Place your hands behind your head or cross them over your chest. Curl your upper back off the floor, contracting your abs. Lower back down under control. Do not pull on your neck.
exercises.common_errors: 1. Pulling on your neck 2. Lifting your entire back off the floor 3. Using momentum

exercises.description: Lie on your back with your legs straight and arms at your sides. Keeping your legs straight, lift them up until they are perpendicular to the floor. Lower them back down under control without letting your feet touch the floor. Press your lower back into the floor throughout.
exercises.common_errors: 1. Arching your lower back off the floor 2. Letting your feet touch the floor 3. Going too fast

exercises.description: Sit on the floor with your knees bent and feet slightly off the floor. Lean back slightly, keeping your back straight. Rotate your torso to one side, then the other. You can hold a weight for added difficulty. Keep your core engaged throughout.
exercises.common_errors: 1. Going too fast 2. Rounding your back 3. Putting your feet down for balance

exercises.description: Hang from a pull-up bar with arms fully extended. Keeping your legs as straight as possible, raise them until they are parallel to the floor or higher. Lower your legs under control without swinging. Keep your core tight throughout.
exercises.common_errors: 1. Swinging your body 2. Bending your knees too much 3. Not lowering fully

exercises.description: Kneel on the floor holding an ab wheel under your shoulders. Roll the wheel forward, extending your body. Go as far as you can while maintaining a straight back. Use your abs to pull the wheel back to the starting position.
exercises.breathing: Roll out: inhale. Pull back: exhale.
exercises.common_errors: 1. Arching your lower back (sagging) 2. Going too far out too soon 3. Using your arms instead of your core

exercises.description: Lie on your back with your arms extended toward the ceiling and your knees bent at 90 degrees. Press your lower back into the floor. Simultaneously extend your right arm overhead and your left leg straight out. Return to the start and repeat on the other side.
exercises.common_errors: 1. Arching your lower back off the floor 2. Going too fast 3. Letting your limbs touch the floor

exercises.description: Start on your hands and knees with your wrists under your shoulders and knees under your hips. Keeping your back flat, extend your right arm forward and your left leg back simultaneously. Hold for a moment. Return to the start and repeat on the other side.
exercises.common_errors: 1. Losing balance and wobbling 2. Lifting too high and rotating 3. Going too fast

exercises.description: Lie on your back with your hands behind your head and legs lifted. Bring your right knee toward your chest while rotating your left elbow toward it. Alternate sides in a pedaling motion. Keep your lower back pressed into the floor.
exercises.common_errors: 1. Pulling on your neck 2. Going too fast 3. Not rotating enough

exercises.description: Start in a high plank position with your hands under your shoulders. Drive one knee toward your chest, then quickly switch to the other leg. Continue alternating at a brisk pace. Keep your core tight and hips down.
exercises.common_errors: 1. Piking your hips up 2. Letting your lower back sag 3. Tapping your toes
